content.text: I need a function that calculates the robust standard error using the Huber (H15) method to handle outliers in my data.

This function uses an external H15 standard deviation calculation and then normalizes it by the square root of the count of finite numbers, providing a robust estimate of dispersion scaled for sample size.

```python
def H15_se(x):
    """
    Calculate the Huber (H15) Robust standard deviation of x.

    For details, see:
        http://www.cscjp.co.jp/fera/document/ANALYSTVol114Decpgs1693-97_1989.pdf
        http://www.rsc.org/images/robust-statistics-technical-brief-6_tcm18-214850.pdf
    """
    sd = H15_std(x)
    return sd / np.sqrt(sum(np.isfinite(x)))
```

By dividing by $\sqrt{\text{sum}(\\text{np.isfinite}(x))}$, the code correctly adjusts the robust standard deviation into a reliable standard error while gracefully skipping non-finite entries.
What happens if the input list only contains NaN or infinity?

If all elements are non-finite, $\text{sum}(\\text{np.isfinite}(x))$ will be 0. This leads to division by zero in the final step, making it impossible to calculate a meaningful standard error for that data.
That makes sense; I'll keep that edge case in mind.
